stimulus: When studying shrimp feeding from hydro-thermal vents at the bottom of the ocean, biologists were surprised that the shrimps' reproductive cycles followed seasonal patterns. Far beyond and their springtime release coincides with a peak in algae raining down from surface waters. So far, researchers have found no evidence of seasonal breeding among vent-dwelling species that provide their offspring with yolk to sustain them or among vent-dwelling species found in areas of the ocean with not seasonal algae blooms.
prompt: Which of the following best describes the function of the highlighted sentence?

options.text: It casts doubt on the accuracy of earlier observations of seasonal breeding among shrimp species living near hydro-thermal vents.
It undermines the explanation proposed for seasonal breeding among some shrimp species living near hydro-thermal vents.
It suggests that alternative theories are needed to explain seasonal breeding among shrimp species living near hydro-thermal vents.
It describes the survival benefits to shrimp of mating in parts of the ocean where algae blooms rain down abundantly.
It supports the explanation proffered for the seasonal breeding observed among some shrimp species living near hydro-thermal vents.

explanation: 正確答案為 E（It supports the explanation proffered for the seasonal breeding observed among some shrimp species living near hydro-thermal vents.）。高亮句其實很像我們平時做題喜歡用的排除法“3個選項，不選A不選B，所以只能選 C”，A是“為後代提供卵黃以維持其生存的季節性繁殖物種”，B是“沒有季節性海藻爆發的海洋地區發現某些魚類進行季節性繁殖”，C是“蝦的繁殖週期遵循季節規律”

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 10（匯入序號 194）· 題目 PDF 第 221 頁

stimulus: In various writings from the 1940s on popular culture, George Orwell examined commercial texts such as comics and crime novels, seeking out political meanings that ran counter to what he considered an inherent tendency toward socialism in the English common people. The public, he concluded, was often being duped by a convert patrician conservatism, conveyed through commercial culture, that restrained the people's radical instincts. These works constituted some of Orwell' s greatest writing, yet those who see him as a lone precursor to today's cultural studies, a field in which scholars examine the ideological implications of popular culture, are mistaken. A number of left-wing writers in the 1930s, many of them associated with the Communist Party, saw the need to take popular culture seriously. The passage suggests
prompt: which of the following about George Orwell?

options.text: He regarded commercial texts a vehicles for the views of ordinary people.
He regarded many commercial texts as having an insidious effect on readers.
He considered commercial text such as comics to be unworthy of serious analysis.
He initiated a new direction in scholarship by taking popular culture seriously.
He regarded commercial texts as inappropriate vehicles for political ideas.

explanation: 正確答案為 B（He regarded many commercial texts as having an insidious effect on readers.）。第1句 GO 開始研究流行文字和公眾的社會主義矛盾性； 第2句 GO認為公眾經常被保守主義欺騙，被抑制了創新本能； 第3句 GO 曾被認是先驅； 第4句有人比GO更早的關注這些。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 11（匯入序號 195）· 題目 PDF 第 222 頁

stimulus: In the country of Seligia where asbestos has been used extensively in construction, that use is about to be banned, because the exposure of construction workers to asbestos can cause a certain type of cancer. We can predict that the ban will result in reducing the incidence of that type of cancer in Seligia by as much as 50 percent. It is true that less than 30 percent of the death certificates of Seligians who have died of that cancer have "construction" listed as the deceased's occupation. The cancer takes about 20 years after exposure to develop, however, and Seligians who have worked in construction commonly turn to less strenuous occupations as they age. Seligian death certificates indicate only the deceased's last occupation. In the argument given, the two highlighted portions play
prompt: which of the following roles?

options.text: The first is a prediction that argument seeks to defend, the second presents evidence that if not supplemented would cast doubt on that judgment.
The first is a prediction that the argument seeks to defend, the second is a finding, the accuracy of which is disputed by the argument as a whole.
The first is a prediction that the argument as a whole rejects the second presents evidence on which that rejection is based
The first is a prediction that the argument as a whole seeks to defend, the second is evidence that the defense can be at best partially successful.
The first is a prediction that is cited in order to justify a stated conclusion, the second is a consideration that has been raised against that conclusion.

explanation: 正確答案為 A（The first is a prediction that argument seeks to defend, the second presents evidence that if not supplemented would cast doubt on that judgment.）、E（The first is a prediction that is cited in order to justify a stated conclusion, the second is a consideration that has been raised against that conclusion.）。第1句很顯然是基於前文第1句而產生的預測，第3句用一組資料來質疑第2句的預測

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 12（匯入序號 196）· 題目 PDF 第 222 頁

stimulus: The damage that trucks do to highways escalates dramatically with the weight borne per axle. New regulations increase the maximum permitted vehicle weight; these regulations will therefore allow onto the highway trucks capable of doing even more damage than those currently permitted.
prompt: The argument is open up to criticism on the grounds that it

options.text: fails to establish that the level of damage done by trucks is a serious problem
omits evidence about one of the two relevant factors
ignores the possibility that careful driving can lessen damage to highways
presumes that trucks are the only vehicles capable of causing substantial damage to highways
presumes that something is bound to happen just because it is allowed to happen

explanation: 正確答案為 B（omits evidence about one of the two relevant factors）。文章顯然前後不對應，前面講的是“每根車軸所承載的重量”，後面卻是“車輛的最高許可重量”，所以文章缺失的是如何把“車輛的最高許可重量”和“每根車軸所承載的重量”進行邏輯對應來找出兩者的必然關係。簡而言之，總重量越重，單個車軸所承載的重量不一定增加；因為我們完全可以多加幾個軸來分散總重啊。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 13（匯入序號 197）· 題目 PDF 第 223 頁

stimulus: Benovians set their clocks back an hour for the winter. The result is that, during winter's short days, it is light when most commuters drive to work, but dark when they drive back home. Darkness contributes to accidents. Changing the clocks, however, does not actually increase the amount of driving done in the dark, so it is unlikely to have any effect on Benovia's automobile accident rate.
prompt: Which of the following is an assumption on which the argument relies?

options.text: The average number of hours that Benovians drive when it is dark is greater for days during the winter than for days during other times of the year.
In Benovia, hazards to safe driving that are made worse by darkness are as likely to occur in the morning as in the evening.
The majority of cars on Benovia's roads during a given day are those of people commuting to or from work.
The majority of automobile accidents in Benovia take place when it is dark.
Driving conditions are no worse in Benovia in the winter than during the rest of the year.

explanation: 正確答案為 B（In Benovia, hazards to safe driving that are made worse by darkness are as likely to occur in the morning as in the evening.）。首先找結論改變時鐘不太可能對 Benovia 的汽車事故率產生任何影響。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是 ABC 或是A 或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 大多數通勤者開車去上班時天色是亮的，而開車回家時天色卻是黑的； B 黑暗會導致事故； C 改變時鐘實際上並不能增加夜間駕駛的次數； 結論改變時鐘不太可能對 Benovia 的汽車事故率產生任何影響。 當調整時間之後：大多數通勤者開車去上班時天色是亮的，而開車回家時天色卻是黑的當調整時間之前：大多數通勤者開車去上班時天色是黑的，而開車回家時天色卻是亮的要保證前後發生事故的機率相等，就必須保證“回家時天黑的事故率=上班時天黑的事故率”，白天的事故率不需要考慮，因為文章的B條件已經限定了晚上。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 18（匯入序號 202）· 題目 PDF 第 225 頁

stimulus: TEB Attorney: TEB Corporation has been accused of not doing enough to discourage its executives from defrauding the government. This accusation is obviously false, since TEB, following its own written policy, rewards those who report the wrongdoing of others and promptly fires proven wrongdoers.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most seriously undermines the attorney's argument in defense of TEB?

options.text: "Wrongdoing" is defined in TEB corporate policies as "any activity by a TEB employee that defrauds TEB."
TEB has instituted what it calls "customer friendly" policies of responding to every complaint
The government is not TEB's sole client, nor even its major client.
Defrauding the government would have to involve more than one employee of any company
Those who have accused TEB of not doing enough to discourage its executives from defrauding the government have themselves been government employees.

explanation: 正確答案為 A（"Wrongdoing" is defined in TEB corporate policies as "any activity by a TEB employee that defrauds TEB."）。文章顯然前後不對應，第1 句講“詐騙”，第2 句卻以“舉報不當行”來搪塞，這裡就相當於認“舉報不當行為”就不會產生“詐騙”，簡而言之“不當行為”=“詐騙”，太過於武斷；詐騙就應該以不是詐騙的證據來自證清白，而不是拿另外一個優勢來證明。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 19（匯入序號 203）· 題目 PDF 第 226 頁

stimulus: The Rivera Art Museum recently began charging admission. The resulting decline in visitors has been far larger than at other local museums, which have also begun charging admission. The magnitude of the decline might be due to the Rivera' s location near government offices. Because an admission charge is most discouraging to those who plan a short visit, it is likely that government workers who formerly made brief visits during lunchtime and after work now
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most seriously undermines the proposed explanation?

options.text: The fee for admission to the Rivera is no larger than that charged by other museums
The Rivera does not keep track of how long individual visitors stay in the museum
The decline in visitors to the Rivera has been no greater in workdays than it has been on nonworking days
The museum with the smallest decline in visitors is the most popular with visitors from other countries
In the period between the announcement that there would be an admissions charge and its actual introduction, there was an increase in visitors at the Rivera.

explanation: 正確答案為 C（The decline in visitors to the Rivera has been no greater in workdays than it has been on nonworking days）。文章顯然前後不對應，第2句事實結論“遊客人數”，第4句原因僅僅“短期遊客”，但是“遊客人數”=“短期遊客”+“非短期遊客”，本文並沒有涉及到“非短期遊客”這個變數

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 20（匯入序號 204）· 題目 PDF 第 226 頁

stimulus: Some conservationists, concerned about the rapid depletion through poachingof elephant herds in certain parts of Africa, are urging countries to which ivory is sold to ban ivory importation. This ban, they argue, would prevent ivory sellers from reaching their market, and elephant herds would have a chance to recover. The conservationists' recommendation as described is based on
prompt: which of the following assumptions?

options.text: The capacity of any illegal channels that may exist for the sale of ivory in export markets cannot expand to meet demand in those markets
There now exist synthetic substitutes for ivory that are essentially indistinguishable from the natural product
Bans on imports of ivory need to be combined with intensified efforts to stop poaching in order to be effective
To the economies of those countries that have significant elephant herds, elephants are more valuable alive than dead
In some parts of Africa where poaching has depleted elephant herds, those herds are already beyond recovery

explanation: 正確答案為 A（The capacity of any illegal channels that may exist for the sale of ivory in export markets cannot expand to meet demand in those markets）。首先找結論這項禁令將阻止象牙銷售商進入他們的市場，大象群將有機會恢復。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是ABC或是A或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。A 偷獵大象群會導致大象數量迅速減少； B 敦促出售象牙的國家禁止象牙進口； 結論這項禁令將阻止象牙銷售商進入他們的市場，大象群將有機會恢復。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 21（匯入序號 205）· 題目 PDF 第 227 頁

stimulus: The surface of Venus contains calcite, a mineral that absorbs the gas sulfur dioxide. Sulfur dioxide is often produced by volcanic activity and is plentiful in Venus' atmosphere, so there must be a source, perhaps volcanic, producing sulfur dioxide on Venus.
prompt: Which of the following is an assumption on which the argument depends?

options.text: In the recent past, the level of sulfur dioxide in Venus' atmosphere was not much higher than it is now.
If there is volcanic activity on the surface of Venus, it is not adequate to maintain the observed amount of sulfur dioxide in the atmosphere.
The sulfur dioxide in Venus' atmosphere forms clouds that prevent direct observation of the surface.
Sulfur dioxide produced by volcanic activity on the Earth's surface is also absorbed by minerals other than calcite.
The calcite on the surface of Venus is in sufficient supply to continue to absorb sulfur for millions of years to come.

explanation: 正確答案為 A（In the recent past, the level of sulfur dioxide in Venus' atmosphere was not much higher than it is now.）。首先找結論金星大氣層中的二氧化硫是火山活動產生的。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是ABC或是A或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 火山通常會產生二氧化硫； 結論金星大氣層中的二氧化硫是火山活動產生的。 第1句提到的方解石能夠吸收二氧化硫，並且二氧化硫含量豐富，那說明目前金星上“產生二氧化硫的速率”大於或等於“方解石吸收速率”。若大於，二氧化硫會越來越多；若等於，二氧化硫數量會不變。總之，過去二氧化硫要麼比現在少，要麼和現在一樣。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 22（匯入序號 206）· 題目 PDF 第 227 頁

stimulus: A mouse's immune system generally rejects proteins different from those produced by that mouse. The immune system of a pregnant mouse does not, however, reject the mouse's fetuses, although the fetal tissue a fetus produces as it develops typically contains many such proteins. Some scientists hypothesize that the placenta surrounding the fetus physically blocks the mother's immune system. But others, noting that the placenta produces IDO, an enzyme that suppresses the immune system, hypothesize that IDO plays a crucial role in protecting the In order to choose between the two hypotheses, it would be most useful to determine
prompt: which of the following?

options.text: Whether mice sometimes produce IDO when they are not pregnant
Whether the immune systems of fetal mice are capable of attacking the tissue of the mother
Whether there are cases in which the immune system of a pregnant mouse rejects some fetuses but not others
Whether IDO is the only substance capable of suppressing the immune system produced by mice
Whether the immune systems of pregnant mice that are given a drug that inhibits IDO production subsequently reject the fetus.

explanation: 正確答案為 E（Whether the immune systems of pregnant mice that are given a drug that inhibits IDO production subsequently reject the fetus.）。首先找出兩個假設的區別。 假設1 胎兒周圍的胎盤會阻礙母親的免疫系統（胎盤親自阻止） 假設2 胎盤會產生IDO，IDO 抑制免疫系統（胎盤派手下去阻止） 所以本題的變數就是IDO，只要對IDO 這個變數進行控制，就能得到想要的結果。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 23（匯入序號 207）· 題目 PDF 第 228 頁

stimulus: In general, naive individuals appear more likely to learn from interaction with familiar rather than unfamiliar members of their species. Kaveliers and colleagues (2005) reported that naive laboratory-bred deer mice showed greater social learning of defensive responses to biting flies after observing responses of familiar members than after observing responses of unfamiliar members. Earlier, Valsecchi and colleagues (1996) reported striking differences in social learning of food preferences among Mongolian gerbils, depending on whether they were exposed to familiar or unfamiliar demonstrators. At the time, this was unexpected because Gaief and colleagues (1984) had previously reported no familiarity effect in Norway rats on social learning of food preferences. However, Gaief et al (1998) subsequently uncovered a significant familiarity effect that was evident when demonstrators are some hours before interacting with observers but absent when demonstrators are immediately before interacting.
prompt: Which of the following can be inferred about the report by Gaief and colleagues (1984) as it is described in the passage?

options.text: It introduced a concept that the report by Kaveliers and colleagues was intended to clarify.
It highlighted an inaccuracy in the report by Valsecchi and colleagues
It led to an incorrect supposition on the part of some scientists
It played an important role in developing an understanding of social learning between members of different species.
It suggested that the familiarity effect is stronger in some species than in others.

explanation: 正確答案為 C（It led to an incorrect supposition on the part of some scientists）。Gaief及其同事（1984）的報告在文章中第4句，也就是那個錯誤的報告，和如今研究結果完全相悖。本題無非是把這個錯誤的報告當做條件，來找一個結論。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 25（匯入序號 209）· 題目 PDF 第 229 頁

stimulus: The crustaceans known as harpacticoids are very widespread in marine sediments, where they feed on microorganisms by ingesting the sediment particles to which the microorganisms adhere. Heavy metals, such as those found in industrial pollution, readily adhere to sediment particles. Harpacticoids are poisoned by heavy metals but are unaffected by most other pollutants. Therefore the concentration of harpacticoids in an area is a good indication of whether that marine environment contains heavy metals.
prompt: Which of the following is an assumption on which the argument relies?

options.text: Industrial pollution is the principal source of heavy metals in marine sediments.
Harpacticoids are the only crustaceans that feed on microorganisms by ingesting sediment particles.
Harpacticoids are more susceptible to poisoning by heavy metals than are other marine organisms.
The microorganisms that harpacticoids feed on are not killed by pollutants that are harmless to harpacticoids.
The microorganisms that harpacticoids feed on absorb heavy metals.

explanation: 正確答案為 D（The microorganisms that harpacticoids feed on are not killed by pollutants that are harmless to harpacticoids.）。首先找結論一個區域內鏈黴菌的濃度可以很好地指示該海洋環境是否含有重金屬。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是ABC或是A 或是ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A Harpacticoid 透過攝取微生物附著的沉積物顆粒來捕食微生物； B Harpacticoid 被重金屬毒害，但不受大多數其他汙染物的影響； 結論一個區域內 Harpacticoid 的數量可以很好地指示該海洋環境是否含有重金屬。 “Harpacticoid 的數量”由兩個己知變數決定，“捕食微生物（會不會餓死）被重金屬毒害”，所以我們只有在控制“捕食微生物（會不會餓死）“變數不變的情況下，才能確定是否含有重金屬，因要排除餓死的因素。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 28（匯入序號 212）· 題目 PDF 第 231 頁

stimulus: A hoard of coins recently unearthed at an ancient Carthagian site bear an image of a face in profile with what appears to be a large mole on the cheek. Pointing out that an artist would be unlikely to include such a specific detail in a generalized portrayal of a face, some archaeologists have concluded that these coins portray an actual individual rather than an idealized type. However, this conclusion is unwarranted, since
prompt: Which of the following most logically completes the argument?

options.text: archaeologists cannot determine the actual identity of the individual portrayed just from the likeness that appears on the coins
it cannot be established with certainty that Carthagian artists were on the whole less skillful at portraying human face than artists of other cultures
none of the other designs of Carthagian coins that have been discovered features a face with a mole on it
in ancient times it was quite common for the money circulated in a city or country to include coins that originated in other jurisdictions.
the relatively soft metal used for Carthagian coin molds sometimes resulted in the production of series of coins with unintended bumps

explanation: 正確答案為 E（the relatively soft metal used for Carthagian coin molds sometimes resulted in the production of series of coins with unintended bumps）。考古學家的結論沒有根據，注意這並不一定是讓我們直接反駁結論。什麼叫根據， 根據就是我們得出結論的依據條件，也就是我們的理由證據，本文的證據條件是藝術家不太可能在一張面部的全貌中包含如此具體的細節，不包含細節=虛擬，包含細節=現實。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 43（匯入序號 227）· 題目 PDF 第 238 頁

stimulus: Before 1970, children in the United States were commonly vaccinated against smallpox. In the early 1970s, large-scale vaccinations of children against smallpox were discontinued because smallpox had virtually disappeared from the world. Recently, some scientists have cautioned that the Unite States could be exposed to smallpox again. Contrary to what many people believe, this exposure would probably find all age-groups within the United States population at comparable levels of risk of contracting smallpox, since
prompt: Which of the following most logically completes the passage?

options.text: smallpox is a highly communicable disease among people who are not immune to it
smallpox vaccine is not now widely available in the United States
the population of the United States has been growing more slowly since 1970 than it was before then
smallpox vaccinations lose their efficacy after about 20 years
people in the United States travel more than they did in 1970, thereby increasing the chances that an epidemic could spread

explanation: 正確答案為 D（smallpox vaccinations lose their efficacy after about 20 years）。第4 句“所有年齡組的人都有患天花的危險”，假設以 2019年基準線，最晚一批接種疫苗的人已經49歲左右了；那我們可以把“全年齡段”拆分為“小於49歲的人沒有接種疫苗”、“大於49 羅的人接種疫苗”，“小於49 羅的人沒有接種疫苗”變數我們完全可以確定他們會受到天花威脅，而“大於 49 歲的人接種疫苗”是本文的未知變數，我們需要對這個變數進行控制。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 47（匯入序號 231）· 題目 PDF 第 240 頁

stimulus: Samples taken from the ice of glaciers show that during the last ice age there was far more dust in the atmosphere than there is now. Since greater amounts of dust in the atmosphere would result in less sunlight's reaching the Earth's surface, it is likely that the large amount of dust then present helped maintain low global temperatures and thus prolonged the ice age.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most seriously weaken the argument?

options.text: Large amount of dust in the atmosphere would tend to keep heat from the Sun that reached the Earth's surface from escaping into outer space.
The glacial samples show that during the period between the two most recent ice ages the level of atmospheric dust was far lower than it was during either of ice ages.
Volcanic eruptions in the recent past have released sufficient dust to cause detectable changes in global temperature patterns.
The amount of dust in the atmosphere was significantly greater in the middle of the last ice age than at the end.
Sunlight is the main heat source of the Earth's surface.

explanation: 正確答案為 A（Large amount of dust in the atmosphere would tend to keep heat from the Sun that reached the Earth's surface from escaping into outer space.）。既然能阻止熱量進入，那同樣也可以阻止熱量散出。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 49（匯入序號 233）· 題目 PDF 第 241 頁

stimulus: Spiders of many species change color to resemble the color of the flowers they sit on. Many animals that prey on such spiders possess color discrimination so acute that they, unlike human beings, can readily see the spiders despite the seeming camouflage. It is unlikely, therefore, that the spiders' color changes confer significant survival benefit on these spiders.
prompt: Which of the following would it be most useful to determine in order to evaluate the argument?

options.text: Whether the spiders' own ability to discriminate color-camouflaged species is as acute as that of their predators
Whether the spiders have a color that could properly be regarded as their natural color
Whether the spiders' color-changes allow them to remain undetected by prey
Whether different species of color-changing spiders tend to favor different species of flower
Whether the color-changing spiders prey on any insects that themselves change color

explanation: 正確答案為 C（Whether the spiders' color-changes allow them to remain undetected by prey）。答案可由文章中的明示資訊、語意關係或作者立場推出。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 52（匯入序號 236）· 題目 PDF 第 243 頁

stimulus: In an experiment, a group of pregnant mice were each given food containing minute doses of the hormone BPA. A control group of pregnant mice were given no BPA. Offspring of mothers that had been given BPA were 10 percent bigger when weaned from their mothers than were offspring of mothers in the control group. Clearly, this result supports the hypothesis that the physical development of mice is significantly affected by prenatal exposure to BPA.
prompt: Which of the following is an assumption on which the argument depends?

options.text: Prenatal exposure of mice to doses of BPA even smaller than those used in the experiment would not have significantly affected the physical development of the mice.
At birth, there was no difference in size between offspring of mothers that had been given BPA and offspring of mothers in the control group.
Postnatal exposure of mice to BPA before the mice are weaned has significant effects on the physical development of the mice.
Exposure of a pregnant mouse to the doses of BPA used in the experiment does not significantly enhance its ability nurse its offspring.
Significant differences in size between offspring of mothers that had been given BPA and offspring of mothers in the control group persisted long after the offspring were weaned

explanation: 正確答案為 B（At birth, there was no difference in size between offspring of mothers that had been given BPA and offspring of mothers in the control group.）。首先找結論小鼠的身體發育受到產前BPA 荷爾蒙的顯著影響。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是ABC或是A 或是ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 服用 BPA的母親的孩子在斷奶時比對照組的孩子大10%； 結論小鼠的身體發育受到產前 BPA荷爾蒙的顯著影響。 斷奶時體型重量=出生重量+出生後的生長發育重量，文章並沒有對“出生重量”這個變數進行描述，就武斷的認為“出生後的生長發育重量”變數受 BPA 的影響；若“出生重量“很大， 那“出生後的生長發育重量”其實並不大。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 54（匯入序號 238）· 題目 PDF 第 244 頁

stimulus: The accepted model of planetary formation holds that Jupiter-sized planets could only form in orbits much farther from their parent star than Earth is from the Sun. Recently, astronomers have detected planets, though as large a Jupiter, have orbits around stars older than the Sun that are much closer to those stars than Earth's orbit is to the Sun. Nonetheless, most astronomers continue to regard the accepted model as correct.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, provides astronomers with the strongest support for continuing to regard the accepted model of planetary formation as correct, despite the new discoveries?

options.text: The instruments used to detect planets orbiting other stars are incapable of detecting planets that are smaller than Jupiter.
Only a small fraction of the stars likely to have planets have been examined so far
After being formed, planets as small as Earth might be attracted to large planets, eventually becoming their satellites.
There is no known reason why older stars would tend to be more likely than younger stars to have planets
Planets tend to drawn into closer orbits of their parent star as time passes.

explanation: 正確答案為 E（Planets tend to drawn into closer orbits of their parent star as time passes.）。首先找到結論木星大小的行星只能在離恆星遠得多的軌道上形成，比地球離太陽還遠。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是 ABC 或是A 或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 雖然它們和木星一樣大，但它們圍繞著比太陽更古老的恆星執行的軌道比地球軌道離太陽更近結論木星大小的行星只能在離恆星遠得多的軌道上形成，比地球離太陽還遠。 本文的條件A和結論給人一種看似矛盾的感覺，但實則兩者在時間上產生了一定的錯層，條件A的時間週期比較長，結論的時間週期比較短，這個時間錯層是我們的突破口。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 55（匯入序號 239）· 題目 PDF 第 244 頁

stimulus: Recently an unusually high number of dolphins have been found dead of infectious diseases, and most of these had abnormally high tissue concentrations of certain compounds that, even in low concentrations, reduce dolphins' resistance to infection. The only source of these compounds in the dolphins' environment is boat paint. Therefore, since dolphins rid their bodies of the compounds rapidly once exposure ceases, their mortality rate should decline rapidly if such boat paints are banned.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most strengthens the argument?

options.text: The levels of the compounds typically used in boat paints today are lower than they were in boat paints manufactured a decade ago.
In high concentrations, the compounds are toxic to many types of marine animals.
The compounds break down into harmless substances after a few months of exposure to water
High tissue levels of the compounds have recently been found in some marine animals, but there is no record of any of those animals dying in unusually large numbers recently.
The compounds do not leach out of the boat paint if the paint is applied exactly in accordance with the manufacturer's directions.

explanation: 正確答案為 C（The compounds break down into harmless substances after a few months of exposure to water）、E（The compounds do not leach out of the boat paint if the paint is applied exactly in accordance with the manufacturer's directions.）。首先找到結論如果禁止使用這種船漆，海豚的死亡率應該會迅速下降。緊接著找 AB（當然有可能是 ABC 或是A或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 某種化合物對海豚有巨大傷害； B 這些化合物的唯一來源是船漆； 結論如果禁止使用這種船漆，海豚的死亡率應該會迅速下降。 船漆可分為兩類“已經進入海域的““將從船隻上繼續進入海域的”，本文第3句結論對“將從船隻上繼續進入海域的“變數進行了詳細描述，但是對“已經進入海域的“變數並沒有合理的說明，所以我們需要對這個變數進行控制。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 58（匯入序號 242）· 題目 PDF 第 246 頁

stimulus: When on an airplane, Consuelo never enjoys movies that have been widely recommended because the poor quality of the picture spoils her enjoyment. Since in no circumstances does she ever enjoy movies that have been widely derided, it follows that she never enjoys movies on airplanes.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, would enable the conclusion of the argument to be properly drawn?

options.text: The only place where Consuelo enjoys widely recommended movies is a movie theater.
Widely recommended movies are never shown on airplane.
If a movie shown on an airplane is not widely derided, then it is invariable widely recommended.
If the picture quality of the movies shown on airplanes was better, Consuelo would enjoy the widely recommended movies.
Some movies are neither widely recommended nor widely derided.

explanation: 正確答案為 C（If a movie shown on an airplane is not widely derided, then it is invariable widely recommended.）。首先找結論 Consuelo 在飛機上不喜歡看電影。緊接著找 AB（當然有可能是ABC 或是A 或是ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 垃圾影片會破壞 Consuelo 的體驗； B Consuelo 不喜歡被廣泛推薦的電影、被廣泛嘲笑的電影； 結論 Consuelo 在飛機上不喜歡看電影。 文章有一個明顯的漏洞，沒有說明飛機上有什麼電影，這是一個關鍵的變數；如果飛機上有一些沒被推薦和嘲諷的電影，那Consuelo 會如何選擇呢？如果飛機上只有被推薦和嘲諷的電影，Consuelo 又會如何選擇呢？

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 59（匯入序號 243）· 題目 PDF 第 246 頁

stimulus: Upon maturity, monarch butterflies travel hundreds of miles from their places of origin and lay their eggs on milkweed. The caterpillars that emerge feed on milkweed and absorb the glycosides in milkweed sap. The specific glycosides present in milkweed differ from region to region within the monarch butterfly's range. Since mature butterflies retain the glycosides, the glycosides in a mature monarch butterfly could be used to determine its place of origin.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most strengthens the argument?

options.text: Mature monarch butterflies do not feed on parts of milkweed that contain glycosides.
The glycosides in milkweed sap are slightly toxic to caterpillars of other species.
The vast majority of the monarch butterflies that are laying eggs in a given region will have traveled there from a single region.
There are substances other than glycosides in milkweed sap that accumulate in a monarch caterpillar and are retained in the body of the mature butterfly.
There are certain glycosides that are found in the sap of all milkweeds, no matter where they grow within the monarch butterfly's range.

explanation: 正確答案為 A（Mature monarch butterflies do not feed on parts of milkweed that contain glycosides.）。首先找到結論成熟帝王蝶的糖苷成分可以用來確定它來自哪裡。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是ABC或是A 或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 在Milkweed上產卵，幼蟲吸收 Milkweed 汁液中的糖苷； B 成熟的蝴蝶體內保留著糖苷成分； 結論成熟帝王蝶的糖苷成分可以用來確定它來自哪裡。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 60（匯入序號 244）· 題目 PDF 第 247 頁

stimulus: Iridium, a hard, whitish metal similar to platinum, is extremely rare on Earth. Extremely high concentrations of iridium on Earth result from only two scenarios: massive volcanic eruptions that release iridium from deep within the Earth and meteorites that shower down on Earth from space. When scientists found concentrations of iridium 30 times higher than normal in rock stratum from 65 million years ago, they concluded that a massive meteor or comet hit the Earth and caused the massive extinction of the dinosaurs.
prompt: Which of the following, if true, most strongly supports the scientist's conclusion?

options.text: Volcanoes massive enough to generate high concentrations of iridium are very rare.
Massive volcanic eruptions occurred frequently 80 million years ago.
Most scientists support the hypothesis that a cosmic impact wiped out the dinosaurs.
The massive extinction that occurred 70 million years ago killed not only the dinosaurs but also 70 percent of all life on Earth.
A comet struck the earth some 120 million years ago, but no widespread extinction occurred

explanation: 正確答案為 A（Volcanoes massive enough to generate high concentrations of iridium are very rare.）。首先找到結論一顆巨大的流星或彗星撞擊了地球。緊接著找AB（當然有可能是 ABC 或是 A或是 ABCD），從而得出一整條邏輯線索。 A 地球上極高濃度的銥只有兩種情況：從地球深處釋放出銥的火山爆發和從太空來到地球上的隕石； B 6500萬年前的岩層中發現銥的濃度是正常濃度的30倍； 結論一顆巨大的流星或彗星撞擊了地球。 這道題較為簡單，因為條件 A 直接提供了兩個變數，而不是像前面那些題目需要我們自己找第二個不確定變數；“隕石、火山”，本文的缺陷就在於並沒有對“火山”變數進行否定，所以我們無法直接判定就是因為“隕石”。

source.title: 老肖新 GRE 閱讀 250 篇 · 授權內容
source.detail: 篇章 61（匯入序號 245）· 題目 PDF 第 247 頁

stimulus: Some psychologists have proposed the hypothesis that children in different cultures have different psychologies, demonstrated, for example, by culture-specific tendencies for the children's behavior to be relatively self-assertive or obedient. However, that hypothesis is incorrect, since the psychological mechanisms by which children acquire behavioral traits are identical for all cultures.
prompt: In the argument, the author's position and the position challenged by the author differ in the criteria they use to distinguish different
explanation: 正確答案為 D（psychologies）。全文都是圍繞心理。

explanation: 正確答案為 C（insular）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 B（astounding）、E（stunning）。since they are not by definition "unforeseeable." Thus, the correct answer is astounding (Choice B) and stunning (Choice E).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The unironic representation of objects from everyday life is (i) ______ serious American art of the twentieth century: "high" artists ceded the straightforward depiction of the (ii) ______ to illustrators, advertisers, and packaging designers.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（missing from）、E（commonplace）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 A（diversity）、D（homogeneity）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

prompt: It is refreshing to read a book about our planet by an author who does not allow facts to be (i) ______ by politics: well aware of the political disputes about the effects of human activities on climate and biodiversity, this author does not permit them to (ii) ______ his comprehensive description of what we know about our biosphere. He emphasizes the enormous gaps in our knowledge, the sparseness of our observations, and the (iii) ______, calling attention to the many aspects of planetary evolution that must be better understood before we can accurately diagnose the condition of our planet.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（overshadowed）、E（obscure）、I（superficiality of our theories）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 10 題 · W: OG, p. 49, #1 (PPI, Test Preview, #4)

prompt: Vain and prone to violence, Caravaggio could not handle success: the more his (i) ______ as an artist increased, the more (ii) ______ his life became.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（eminence）、D（tumultuous）。since neither of the other choices suggests being out of control. And for blank (i), the best choice is "eminence," since to increase in eminence is a consequence of becoming more successful. It is true that Cara vaggio might also increase in notoriety, but an increase in notoriety as an artist is not as clear a sign of success as an increase in eminence. Thus the correct answer is eminence (Choice C) and tumultuous (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 11 題 · t: OG, p. 50, #2 (PPII, Test Preview, #5)

prompt: A newly published, laudatory biography of George Bernard Shaw fails, like others before it, to capture the essence of his personality: the more he is (i) ______, the more his true self seems to (ii) ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（discussed）、D（disappear）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 12 題 · te: PBT, p. 52, #5

prompt: A misconception frequently held by novice writers is that sentence structure mirrors thought: the more convoluted the structure, the more ______ the ideas.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（complicated）、F（involved）。Because the second half of the sentence illustrates the idea that "structure mirrors thought," any word that fills the blank must be similar in meaning to "convoluted." The two words that are similar to "convoluted" are "complicated" and "involved" (Choices A and F), which produce sentences alike in meaning. "Fanciful," while somewhat similar in meaning to "convoluted," is not as similar to either "complicated" or "involved" as those words are to each other. The other answer choices are not similar in meaning to "convoluted," and thus do not produce coherent sentences. Thus the correct answer is complicated (Choice A) and involved (Choice F). 32

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 13 題 · He : OG, p. 89, H8, Medium.

prompt: Belanger dances with an (i) ______ that draws one's attention as if by seeking to (ii) ______ it; through finesse and understatement, he manages to seem at once intensely present and curiously detached.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（undemonstrative panache）、F（deflect）。the sentence is to emphasize contradictory aspects of Belanger's dancing: we are told, for example, that he seems "at once intensely present and curiously detached." Looking at the second blank with this point in mind, we can see that the sentence is saying that Belanger draws attention in some way that would not normally be a means of doing so. The only choice that fits, therefore, is "deflect"; focusing or overwhelming attention would certainly be expected to draw it. And since employing "unrestrained enthusiasm" or "unattractive gawkiness" would not be ways of deflecting attention, the correct choice for the first blank is "undemonstrative panache," another paradoxical term, since "panache" means "dash or flamboyance in style." Thus, the correct answer is undemonstrative panache (Choice A) and deflect (Choice F). 34

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The dog's appearance of ______ became increasingly irritating; his whines became more wheedling, his manner more imploring.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（supplication）、D（entreaty）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: With the 1985 discovery of an ozone hole over Antarctica, an international ban on the production of chlorofluorocarbons—implicated in causing the ozone hole began to appear ______ especially since chemical companies' opposition to such a ban had weakened.
explanation: 正確答案為 D（imminent）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 18 題 · : PPII, Practice Test 1, V2, Easy, #2

prompt: Only with the discovery of an ozone hole over Antarctica in 1985 did chemical companies finally relinquish their opposition to a ban on chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), which destroy ozone. The discovery suggested that strong political action to halt production of CFCs might be (i) ______, and fortunately, the chemical industry no longer felt compelled to oppose such action: although companies had recently (ii) ______ their research into CFC substitutes, studies they had initiated years earlier had produced (iii) ______ results.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（imminent）、F（curtailed）、G（encouraging）。Since the completed third blank now indicates that studies of CFC substitutes have been successful, "curtailed" makes the most sense in the second blank. "Corroborated" and "publicized" do not contrast appropriately with the success of the studies. Thus, the correct answer is imminent (Choice A); curtailed (Choice F); and encouraging (Choice G).

Instead, the professor argued that this impulse to (ii) ______ even obviously (iii) ______ views in order to furnish opposing perspectives is harmful to basic accuracy.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（objectivity）、E（present）、H（fringe）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

It was perhaps only a matter of time before someone took advantage of the (iii) ______ to peddle newly created "vintage" prints for profit.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（forgery）、D（ballooned）、G（discrepancy）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 B（problematic）、E（unsound）、H（discredited）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

prompt: This book's strengths are the author's breadth of knowledge and the blending of ideas and findings from many disciplines, including history, the arts and the sciences. Ideas from diverse perspectives are (i) ______ to provide a historical and cross-cultural understanding. But a weakness of the book is its (ii) ______: sometimes there are leaps from one domain to another that (iii) ______ the reader's ability to synthesize a coherent view of our current understanding of this subject.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（interwoven）、D（organization）、I（undercut）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 26 題 · HA: PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Easy, #6

prompt: Always circumspect, she was reluctant to make judgments, but once arriving at a conclusion, she was ______ in its defense.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（intransigent）、F（resolute）。The key phrases that indicate how the blank for this question should be completed are: "circumspect," "reluctant," and "but once." Taken together they point to completing the blank with something that is opposite in some way to the two cited adjectives. Among the answer choices, "intransigent" and "resolute," although not strictly synonymous, both fit the logic of the description given here for completing the blank and create sentences that are similar in meaning. "Lax" and "negligent" are clearly similar in meaning and would create sentences similar in meaning, but they continue the sentiment voiced in the initial clause rather than contrasting with it. "Deferential" and "obsequious" are also similar in meaning, but their emphasis on "politeness," while not strictly synonymous with reluctance and circumspection, like "lax" and "negligent" fail to pick up on the expected contrast. Thus, the correct answer is intransigent (Choice B) and resolute (Choice F). 45

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The slower-learning monkeys searched ______ but unintelligently: although they worked closely together, they checked only the most obvious hiding places.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（cooperatively）、F（harmoniously）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 29 題 · #I : PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Medium, #12

prompt: The plan, which the engineers said would save the aquifer by reducing pumping to ______ levels, has passed a governmental environmental review but faces opposition from outdoor and environmental groups.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（innocuous）、F（benign）。because they imply that the current levels are not feasible or practicable, conflicting with the implication that the current levels, though perhaps undesirable, are nevertheless entirely feasible. Thus, the correct answer is innocuous (Choice A) and benign (Choice F).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Overlarge, uneven, and ultimately disappointing, the retrospective exhibition seems too much like special pleading for a forgotten painter of real but ______ talents.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（limited）、D（circumscribed）。The sentence is explaining why the exhibition of the painter's work was unsatisfactory, and since it says that the painter's talents were real, the word in the blank has to indicate why those talents were not, in the opinion of the author of the sentence, good enough. The words "limited" and "circumscribed" do so and also produce sentences that are alike in meaning, so this pair forms the correct answer. Although "undiscovered" and "hidden" are similar in meaning, they do not make sense in the context of the sentence, since they do not indicate why the painter's talents were not adequate. Other choices, such as "partial" and "prosaic" might make sense in context, but none of the other choices that meets that criterion also has a companion choice that would produce another sentence alike in meaning. Thus the correct answer is limited (Choice A) and circumscribed (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

explanation: 正確答案為 A（be keen on）、B（be inclined to）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 34 題 · Ze: PPII, Practice Test 1, V2, Hard, #13

prompt: Certain music lovers yearn for (i) ______, but when it is achieved, there is something missing; perhaps they feel uncomfortable in a world where nothing discernible is (ii) ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（flawlessness）、D（wrong）。the sentence alerts us that it will describe a kind of contradiction or paradox: music lovers want something, but when they get what they want, they discover some cause for dissatisfaction. Among the answer choices, the only two words that produce such a paradox are "flawlessness" and "wrong" the music lovers long for flawlessness but are unsatisfied with a world in which nothing is wrong. Thus, the correct answer is flawlessness (Choice C) and wrong (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 35 題 · Ja: OG, p. 498, #5

prompt: Because we assume the (i) ______ of natural design, nature can often (ii) ______ us: as the Wright brothers noted, the birds initially misled them in almost every particular, but their Flyer eventually succeeded by being the least avian of the early flying machines.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（preeminence）、F（beguile）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 36 題 · 4A: PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Easy, #4

prompt: In her later years, Bertha Pappenheim was an apostle of noble but already (i) ______ notions, always respected for her integrity, her energy, and her resolve but increasingly out of step and ultimately (ii) ______ even her own organization.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（anachronistic）、F（alienated from）。The sentence is clearly conveying a contrast since "but" is used twice to indicate something positive and something negative about Pappenheim. The clue to the negative aspect is in the later part of the sentence, where "out of step" leads both to "anachronistic" as the answer for the first blank and "alienated from" as the answer for the second. Thus, the correct answer is anachronistic (Choice A) and alienated from (Choice F).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 38 題 · te: OG, p. 370, #5

prompt: The traditional gap between theorists and experimentalists is (i) ______ as one of haughty high priests versus pugnacious pragmatists. But the reality is more often that one researcher does not understand another's jargon, or is afraid to ask stupid questions, or is hesitant about giving up vital and hard-won information to a distant colleague. These were the kinds of (ii) ______ that Dr. Sonnenwald was able to (iii) ______, leading to greater cooperation between theorists and experimentalists.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（often caricatured）、F（barriers）、H（dismantle）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 40 題 · : PPII, Practice Test 1, V2, Medium, #5

prompt: Political advertising may well be the most (i) ______ kind of advertising: political candidates are actually quite (ii) ______, yet their campaign advertisement often hide important differences behind smoke screens of smiles and empty slogans.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（deceptive）、E（dissimilar）。Looking at Blank (i), it is hard to select a correct answer, since all three answer choices fit the immediate context well. Looking to the second part of the sentence, however, we can see such expressions as "hide" and "smoke screens," both of which suggest that the correct answer for Blank (i) is "deceptive." Making that assumption, we can go on to see that the answer for Blank (ii) is "dissimilar," since what is deceptive about political advertisements is that they hide important differences. Reading the sentence again with "deceptive" and "dissimilar" in place confirms those choices. Thus the correct answer is deceptive (Choice C) and dissimilar (Choice E). 58 7-2 although (though)

explanation: 正確答案為 C（enfeeble）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 E（clear）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

prompt: The judge's standing in the legal community, though shaken by phony allegations of wrongdoing, emerged, at long last, ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（undiminished）、D（undamaged）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 47 題 · He : OG, p. 79, #7, Easy

prompt: Unlike the problems in recent financial scandals, issues raised by the regulators in this case appear largely to pertain to unwieldy accounting rules that are open to widely divergent interpretations—not to (i) ______ transactions designed to (ii) ______ corporate malfeasance.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（sham）、D（cloak）。the sentence and the "not to" that follows the dash set up a contrast between the relatively innocent problems in the current case and the issues involved in the "recent financial scandals." Clearly, these latter issues must have involved wrongdoing. Looking at the second blank, only transactions designed to "cloak" corporate malfeasance would qualify: both ameliorating and illuminating malfeasance are positive actions. For the first blank, only "sham" fits; "unpremeditated" or "justifiable" transactions could not be designed to cloak malfeasance. Thus, the correct answer is sham (Choice A) and cloak (Choice D). 64 7-4 rather than

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: This filmmaker is not outspoken on political matters: her films are known for their aesthetic qualities rather than for their ______ ones.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（polemical）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 49 題 · We : PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Medium, #2

prompt: The most striking thing about the politician is how often his politics have been (i) ______ rather than ideological, as he adapts his political positions at any particular moment to the political realities that constrain him. He does not, however, piously (ii) ______ political principles only to betray them in practice. Rather, he attempts in subtle ways to balance his political self-interest with a (iii) ______ viewing himself as an instrument of some unchanging higher purpose.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（strategic）、D（brandish）、H（deeply felt moral code）。Since the politician is portrayed as adapting political positions to political realities, blank (i) should be filled with "strategic," which is also the only choice that provides the required contrast with "ideological." The second blank, brandishing political principles is what a politician might do piously, while flouting is not pious and following principles does not make sense when combined with "betray[ing] them in practice." The third blank requires something that would have to be balanced against "political self-interest" and that would be embraced in service of an "unchanging higher purpose," making "deeply felt moral code" the only viable choice. Thus, the correct answer is strategic (Choice C), brandish (Choice D), and deeply felt moral code (Choice H). 68 7-5 despite (in spite of)

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Critics charge that the regulatory agency, having never defined what constitutes an untenable risk, has grown (i) ______ outside influences on that issue: several experts have (ii) ______ it recently for allowing one power plant to delay an inspection for more than six weeks despite compelling safety concerns.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（susceptible to）、E（panned）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 C（significant）、D（considerable）。the sentence to make sense. Thus the correct answer is significant (Choice C) and considerable (Choice D). 71

The "even the" followed by positive characteristics indicates that the actors did not meet that challenge; "adequate to" is the only answer choice that conveys this sense. Thus, the correct answer is adequate to (Choice D). 74

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 61 題 · Z: PPII, Practice Test 1, V2, Hard, #12

prompt: Notwithstanding their ______ regarding other issues, township residents have consistently passed the board of education's annual budget.
explanation: 正確答案為 D（disagreement）、E（divergence）。the sentence sets up a contrast between the township residents' behavior regarding the "other issues" and their behavior regarding the board's annual budget, which they have "consistently passed." "Accord" and "consensus" are similar in meaning but do not provide the required contrast. Only "disagreement" and "divergence" provide the necessary contrast and lead to two sentences nearly alike in meaning. "Indecision" fits the context, but there is no other word among the possible choices that matches it closely. Thus, the correct answer is disagreement (Choice D) and divergence (Choice E). 77

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Though somewhat less (i) ______ than previous chapters and suffering from a minor rash of academic jargon, the final chapter of the book is nonetheless (ii) ______ laypeople.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（coherent）、E（accessible to）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Far from being ______ the corporate world because of cutbacks, serious researchers are playing a growing role in innovation at many firms.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（banished from）。the correct answer will create a contrast with the idea that "serious researchers are playing a growing role in innovation." Being "banished from" the corporate world and playing a growing role in it are strongly contrasted. Choices A, B, D, and E do not create any such contrast. Thus, the correct answer is banished from (Choice C).

prompt: Richard M. Russell said 52 percent of the nation's growth since the Second World War had (i) ______ invention. He said, (ii) ______ research, the government's greatest role in assuring continuing innovation is promoting a strong, modern patent office. "Unless we can (iii) ______ original ideas, we will not have invention," Mr. Russell said. Speculating on the state of innovation over the next century, several inventors agreed that the future lay in giving children the tools to think creatively and the motivation to invent.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（come through）、E（aside from supporting）、H（protect）。the correct answer is come through (Choice C), aside from supporting (Choice E), and protect (Choice H). 86 8-5 surprising, amazing, astonishing, astounding

But perhaps it is the artistic skill with which the portraits are painted that (ii) ______ their presence in art museums.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（surprising）、E（justifies）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The brief survey, published under the title The Work of Nature: How the Diversity of Life Sustains Us, is surprisingly (i) ______. Indeed it makes several longer treatments of the effects of lost biodiversity seem (ii) ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（comprehensive）、D（redundant）。the sentence again with "comprehensive" and "redundant" filling the blanks confirms that these two choices result in a coherent whole. Thus, the correct answer is comprehensive (Choice C) and redundant (Choice D). 88 8-6 anythin but (anything other than)

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Only by ignoring decades of mismanagement and inefficiency could investors conclude that a fresh infusion of cash would provide anything other than a ______ solution to the company's financial woes.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（fleeting）、C（momentary）。The key phrases that indicate how the blank for this question should be completed are "Only by ignoring decades of mismanagement and inefficiency" and "provide anything other than." Taken together, these phrases indicate that the sentence will not envision a very beneficial or successful resolution of the "financial woes." Among the answer choices, "complete" and "total" are quite close in meaning and would clearly create two sentences very similar in meaning. But those two sentences would be internally contradictory, suggesting that doing something unwise would completely solve a problem. "Fleeting" and "momentary" suggest that the event referred to ("a fresh infusion of cash") might have some beneficial effect, but that it would ultimately not resolve the problem. Thus, the correct answer is fleeting (Choice B) and momentary (Choice C). 89 8-7 belie, antithesis, anomaly

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

explanation: 正確答案為 C（multifaceted）、F（extraneous）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 75 題 · We : PPII, Practice Test 1, V2, Easy, #3

prompt: Given the flood of information presented by the mass media, the only way for someone to keep abreast of the news is to rely on ______ accounts.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（synoptic）、B（abridged）。The key phrase that indicates how the blank for this question should be completed is "the only way for someone to keep abreast of the news." Among the answer choices, "synoptic" and "abridged," while not synonymous in the strict sense, both fit the logic of this description, "synoptic" because of its emphasis on breadth and generality as opposed to detail, and "abridged" because of its obvious focus on brevity. "Sensational" and "lurid" would create two similar sentences but do not fit the logic for completing the blank, since we would not be relying on sensational or lurid accounts in order to keep abreast of the news. Thus, the correct answer is synoptic (Choice A) and abridged (Choice B). 93

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Given how (i) ______ the shortcomings of the standard economic model are in its portrayal of human behavior, the failure of many economists to respond to them is astonishing. They continue to fill the journals with yet more proofs of yet more (ii) ______ theorems. Others, by contrast, accept the criticisms as a challenge, seeking to expand the basic models to embrace a wider range of things people do.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（patent）、E（improbable）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 79 題 · # : PPII, Practice Test 1, V1, Medium, #3

prompt: There may be a threshold below which blood pressure reductions become ______ given that a long-running study showed no decreased heart risk for drops in blood pressure below a certain point.
explanation: 正確答案為 E（superfluous）。the sentence that begins with "given that" provides a reason for a conclusion reached in the first part of the sentence. Since the study "showed no decreased heart risk for drops in blood pressure below a certain point," that point may be a threshold below which reductions in blood pressure provide no benefit; that is, they may be "superfluous." Thus, the correct answer is superfluous (Choice E). 96

explanation: 正確答案為 C（recapitulates）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 84 題 · # : PPII, Practice Test 1, V1, Medium, #1

prompt: The activists' energetic work in the service of both woman suffrage and the temperance movement in the late nineteenth century (i) ______ the assertion that the two movements were (ii) ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（undermines）、E（inimical）。The sentence is about the implications of the activists' energetic work for some assertion about the woman suffrage and temperance movements. The second blank, however, obscures the nature of that assertion. But it is clear that the "energetic work" could either support an assertion that the two movements were similar, or undermine an assertion that the two movements were opposed. "Supports" is offered as a choice for the first blank (as is the somewhat similar "underscores"), but there is no corresponding term in the second blank, nothing along the lines of "similar" or "compatible." "Undermines" and "inimical" make for the only meaningful statement. Thus, the correct answer is undermines (Choice A) and inimical (Choice E).

explanation: 正確答案為 B（settled）、E（ambiguity）、G（similarly equivocal）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 C（impudence）、E（courteous）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 93 題 · Z/ : PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Medium, #4

prompt: In stark contrast to his later (i) ______ Simpson was largely (ii) ______ politics during his college years, despite the fact that the campus he attended was rife with political activity.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（activism）、E（indifferent to）。The sentence tells us that there is a contrast between the way Simpson related to politics in his college years and how he related to politics later in life. So the choices that complete the blanks must contrast with each other. The part of the sentence beginning with "despite" indicates that Simpson's relation to politics in his college years did not involve engagement in the political activity that was "rife." Of the choices for Blank (ii), only "indifferent to" conveys that nonengagement. And of the choices for Blank (i), only "activism" supplies the required contrast with "indifferent to." Thus the correct answer is activism (Choice A) and indifferent to (Choice E). 110 11 НАИТАН :

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: In parts of the Arctic, the land grades into the landfast ice so ______ that you can walk off the coast and not know you are over the hidden sea.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（imperceptibly）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 97 題 · 4A: 0G, p. 50, #3 (PPII, Test Preview, #6)

prompt: The novelist devotes so much time to avid descriptions of his characters' clothes that the reader soon feels that such ______ concerns, although worthy of attention, have superseded any more directly literary aims.
explanation: 正確答案為 D（sartorial）。Although these concerns could also be described as "irrelevant" or "frivolous," neither of these choices is correct because the sentence identifies the concerns as "worthy of attention." Thus, the correct answer is sartorial (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Just as the authors' book on eels is often a key text for courses in marine vertebrate zoology, their ideas on animal development and phylogeny ______ teaching in this area.
explanation: 正確答案為 D（inform）。the sentence should somehow parallel the idea presented in the first half (i.e., the idea that the authors' book on eels is a "key text" in marine vertebrate zoology). Among the choices given, "inform" is clearly the best choice. "Prevent" and "defy" work in the opposite direction, while "use" and "replicate" would suggest that the authors' ideas are drawing upon the teaching in this area rather than the other way around. "Inform" leads to a meaning that nicely matches the first half of the sentence. Thus, the correct answer is inform (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Mechanisms develop whereby every successful species can ______ its innate capacity for population growth with the constraints that arise through its interactions with the natural environment.
explanation: 正確答案為 E（reconcile）。the sentence indicates that the blank should be filled with a verb that indicates what a successful species does with its "innate capacity for population growth" in the face of certain constraints on that growth. This analysis suggests that the correct answer will have something to do with adjusting that capacity in the face of these constraints. Of the choices given, "reconcile" is closest to that meaning. None of the other options make for a meaningful, coherent sentence. "Enhance," for example, may fit nicely with "its innate capacity," but it does not make sense with "constraints." Thus, the correct answer is reconcile (Choice E).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The belief that politicians might become ______ after their election to office led to the appointment of ethics officers at various levels of government.
explanation: 正確答案為 C（venal）。Although several of the other choices are not necessarily positive characteristics, none of them involves ethics. Thus, the correct answer is venal (Choice C). 118 VersaTb

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Human nature and long distances have made exceeding the speed limit a (i) ______ in the state, so the legislators surprised no one when, acceding to public demand, they (ii) ______ increased penalties for speeding.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（cherished tradition）、F（rejected）。since there is nothing in the sentence to suggest that speeding here is "controversial" or "disquieting." In Blank (ii) we need to consider what the legislature would do that would surprise no one with regard to increased penalties for speeding. Given what we have learned so far, "rejected" is the best answer; it would be surprising if the legislature "endorsed" or even "considered" increased penalties for speeding. Thus the correct answer is cherished tradition (Choice B) and rejected (Choice F). 124 10-4 17F55K4

explanation: 正確答案為 A（erstwhile）、E（onetime）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 C（precedence）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Though feminist in its implications, Yvonne Rainer's 1974 film ______ the filmmaker's active involvement in feminist politics.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（antedated）、D（preceded）。the sentence: Rainer's 1974 film exhibits feminist themes in a limited way because it came before she became active in feminist politics. Thus, the correct answer is antedated (Choice A) and preceded (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Wills argues that certain malarial parasites are especially (i) ______ because they have more recently entered humans than other species and therefore have had (ii) ______ time to evolve toward (iii) ______. Yet there is no reliable evidence that the most harmful Plasmodium species has been in humans for a shorter time than less harmful species.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（malignant）、E（insufficient）、H（benignity）。the correct answer is malignant (Choice B), insufficient (Choice E), and benignity (Choice H). 129

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: Ironically, the writer so wary of (i) ______ was (ii) ______ with ink and paper, his novel running to 2,500 shagreen-bound folio pages—a fortune in stationery at the time.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（extravagance）、F（profligate）。the sentence provides most of the context needed to fill in the two blanks. The novel was extremely long and required vast amounts of paper. Among the choices for the second blank, only "profligate" matches this lack of restraint. The word "Ironically" indicates that what the writer was "wary of" was something similar to profligacy; of the choices for the first blank, "extravagance" is the closest. Thus, the correct answer is extravagance (Choice B) and profligate (Choice F). 130

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The demands of ______ notwithstanding, a page or two in Dahl's recent book on democracy that considered what public-choice economics has to say about "democratic failure" or at least a clear signpost to that literature would have been very well spent.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（brevity）、D（economy）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

prompt: Alkan steadfastly refused to ______ her responsibilities as an author: for her an author had to be fearless and committed, and she was always both.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（shirk）、F（neglect）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 118 題 · He : PPII, Practice Test 2, V2, Easy, #12

prompt: The unexplained digressions into the finer points of quantum electrodynamics are so ______ that even readers with a physics degree would be wise to keep a textbook handy to make sense of them.
explanation: 正確答案為 D（frustrating）。the sentence suggests a different interpretation. According to the sentence, it would be "wise to" make sense of the digressions, and a textbook would help the reader to do so. If the digressions are "uninteresting," "unsophisticated," or "humorless," the sentence provides no reason to think it would be wise to make sense of them, and if they are "controversial," it provides no reason to think that a textbook would help. Only if the digressions are "frustrating" does the sentence make a coherent whole. Thus, the correct answer is frustrating (Choice D). 136

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: It is his dubious distinction to have proved what nobody would think of denying, that Romero at the age of sixty-four writes with all the characteristics of ______.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（maturity）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

explanation: 正確答案為 C（dubious）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: The incipient (i) ______ regarding taxes could affect trade between the two countries much more than the (ii) ______ banana imports, which has been going on for years. Unfortunately, the trade regulators seem to be ignoring both disagreements.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（row）、F（festering dispute over）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 124 題 · W: OG, p. 499, #8

prompt: The government has no choice but to (i) ______ the incessant demands for land reform, and yet any governmental action that initiated land reform without requisite attention to agrarian reform would (ii) ______ the overall goal of economic modernization.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（heed）、F（compromise 140）。The sentence informs us that the government has an "overall goal," and the use of "and yet" indicates that initiating land reform without attending to agrarian reform would have some negative consequence for that goal. Thus, the only answer choice that fits the second blank is "compromise." The use of "and yet" also implies that the government's response to the demands for land reform must be in line with initiating such reform, so Choice C, "silence," is incorrect. The description of the demands as "incessant" implies that the demands already exist, so "anticipate" is incorrect. Only "heed" describes an appropriate response. Thus, the correct answer is heed (Choice B) and compromise (Choice F).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 125 題 · t: OG, p. 497, #4

prompt: Like Bela Bartok, Ruth Crawford not only brought a composer's acumen to the notation of folk music, she also had a marked (i) ______ the task. This was clear in her agonizing over how far to try to represent the minute details of a performance in a written text, and this (ii) ______ makes her work a landmark in ethnomusicology.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（reverence for）、D（fastidiousness）。since neither "detachment from" nor "curiosity about" reflects the degree of care Crawford took. Thus the correct answer is reverence for (Choice A) and fastidiousness (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 126 題 · # : OG, p. 86, #2, Medium

prompt: The (i) ______ of Vladimir Nabokov as one of North America's literary giants has thrown the spotlight on his peripheral activities and has thus served to (ii) ______ his efforts as an amateur entomologist.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（lionization）、D（foreground）。since both of those activities represent a turning away from him, not a calling attention to him. So the correct answer for the first blank is "lionization," since to lionize means to treat as important. Then, since we are told that Nabokov is a literary giant, entomology must be one of his peripheral activities, so the correct answer for the second blank must be "foreground," which also means "call attention to." Spotlighting something would not result in transcending it or obscuring it, so neither of the other choices is correct. Thus, the correct answer is lionization (Choice B) and foreground (Choice D).

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

prompt: There is nothing quite like this movie, and indeed I am not altogether sure there is much more to it than its lovely (i) ______. At a moment when so many films strive to be as (ii) ______ as possible, it is gratifying to find one that is so subtle and puzzling.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（peculiarity）、E（assertive）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容

In his analysis, the university culture is largely (i) ______ entering students because academic culture fails to make connections to the kinds of arguments and cultural references that students grasp. Understandably, many students view academic life as (ii) ______ ritual.
explanation: 正確答案為 B（opaque to）、D（an arcane）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

In one of these, the vanquished manage to (i) ______ the victor's triumph as the result of some spurious advantage, the victors being truly inferior where it counts. Often the winners (ii) ______ this interpretation, worrying about the cultural or moral costs of their triumph and so giving some credence to the losers' story.
explanation: 正確答案為 A（construe）、F（collude in）。這些詞能使上下文的轉折、因果與語氣保持一致，且符合空格的文法功能。

source.title: 麟渡兮 GRE 填空 · 授權內容
source.detail: 解答本第 132 題 · t: PPII, Practice Test 2, V1, Medium, #4

stimulus: In 1988 Mr. Smith's annual income was greater than Mrs. Smith's annual income. In 1989 Mr. Smith's annual income decreased by p percent, whereas Mrs. Smith's annual income increased by p percent (p > 0).
prompt: Compare Quantity A and Quantity B.
quantityA: Mr. and Mrs. Smith's combined annual income in 1988
quantityB: Mr. and Mrs. Smith's combined annual income in 1989

options.text: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given.

explanation: 設史密斯先生與太太在 1988 年的收入分別為 X、Y，且 X > Y。1989 年合計收入為 X(1-p%)+Y(1+p%)=X+Y-p%(X-Y)，因 p>0 且 X-Y>0，所以 1989 年合計收入較低，Quantity A 較大。

quantityB: The value of machine Y after 6 years

options.text: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given.

explanation: 兩台機器每年都減損原始價值的 10%。三年後 X 的價值為 V-3(0.1V)=0.7V；六年後 Y 的價值為 2V-6(0.2V)=0.8V，因此 Quantity B 較大。
